succession_and_inheritance.Section 6: Devolution of interest in coparcenary property. Daughter of a coparcener shall by birth become a coparcener in her own right in the same manner as the son. Daughter has the same rights in the coparcenary property as she would have had if she had been a son, including right to claim partition
succession_and_inheritance.Section 8: General rules of succession in case of males. Property of a Hindu male dying intestate devolves firstly upon Class I heirs (son, daughter, widow, mother), then Class II heirs (father, siblings, grandparents), then agnates, then cognates
succession_and_inheritance.Section 9: Order of succession among heirs in the Schedule. Among Class I heirs, the property is distributed equally. Mother gets equal share as each son and daughter
succession_and_inheritance.Section 10: Distribution of property among heirs in Class I of the Schedule. Sons, daughters and widow each take one share. Heirs in branch of pre-deceased son or daughter take the share of the deceased
succession_and_inheritance.Section 14: Property of a female Hindu to be her absolute property. Any property possessed by a female Hindu shall be held by her as full owner thereof and not as a limited owner
succession_and_inheritance.Section 15: General rules of succession in case of female Hindus dying intestate. Devolution upon sons and daughters first, then heirs of husband, then mother and father, then heirs of father, then heirs of mother
succession_and_inheritance.Section 16: Order of succession and manner of distribution among heirs of a female Hindu
succession_and_inheritance.Section 22: Preferential right to acquire property in certain cases. Where an intestate has left a dwelling house with a female heir and male heir, the female heir shall be entitled to the right of residence
succession_and_inheritance.Section 23: Special provision respecting dwelling houses. Where a Hindu intestate has left surviving a female heir and a male heir, the right of the female heir to claim partition of the dwelling house shall not arise until the male heirs choose to divide their shares. Amended to give equal rights
succession_and_inheritance.Section 30: Testamentary succession. Any Hindu may dispose of by will or other testamentary disposition any property which is capable of being so disposed of by him according to law
